Oracle Events
- Search emails for Chloe's DOT compliance message to Blessing using search_emails (query: "DOT" or "compliance" or "placarding" or "Tanaka" or "laser"). Find: Chloe's email detailing the missing hazmat placarding certificate for the Keiko Tanaka move (Mosaic Robotics, Columbus OH to Seattle WA), the Epilog Fusion Pro laser cutter with Class 3B CO2 tube, $59,017/day penalty exposure under 49 CFR 107.329, and the April 23 deadline.
- Search emails for Greg Pallone's reply about the Tanaka shipment using search_emails (query: "Greg" or "Tanaka" or "Swift" or "shipment docs"). Find: Greg's email where he references the WRONG route - Columbus OH to Portland OR instead of Columbus OH to Seattle WA. Agent must notice this discrepancy by cross-referencing against Airtable or CRM data.
- Look up the Keiko Tanaka relocation record in Airtable using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records (query: "Tanaka" or "Keiko"). Find: record confirming the correct route is Columbus to SEATTLE (not Portland), equipment is the Epilog Fusion Pro 48, coordinator is Javier Morales (not Blessing - but Blessing is referenced in the CRM deal as having given verbal confirmation on placarding).
- Search Linear for DOT compliance tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "DOT" or "compliance" or "placarding" or "Tanaka"). Find: (a) Parent issue for Tanaka DOT docs - status IN PROGRESS, missing placarding certificate, assigned to Chloe; (b) Subtask to obtain retroactive cert from Swift - URGENT; (c) Issue description noting that Blessing received verbal confirmation from the Swift driver that placards were affixed but never followed up to obtain the signed certificate document.
- Look up Greg Pallone's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Greg" or "Pallone" or "Swift"). Find: greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com.
- Send email to Greg Pallone requesting the signed DOT placarding certificate using send_email (to: greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com). Email must reference the CORRECT shipment details: Keiko Tanaka, Mosaic Robotics, Columbus OH to SEATTLE WA (not Portland), Epilog Fusion Pro 48 laser cutter, Class 3B CO2 tube, picked up around April 15. Must correct Greg's route confusion from his earlier email.
- Search Linear for the DOT compliance audit project using linear_list_issues (query: "DOT" or "hazmat" or "compliance audit"). Find the four DOT documentation issues: (a) Mei-Lin Zhao / GreenStack / Class 9 Lithium - DONE, all verified; (b) Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic / Class 3B Laser - IN PROGRESS, MISSING placarding cert; (c) Raj Patel / NorthWind / Lithium UPS - DONE, all verified; (d) Joaquin Reyes / GreenStack / Oversize Rig - DONE, not hazmat-classified but included for completeness. Agent should report that 3 of 4 are fully documented and only the Tanaka shipment has a gap.
- Search emails for Emeka's communication with Tamara Byrd using search_emails (query: "Tamara" or "Okoye" or "prototype" or "damage"). Find: Emeka's formal incident report to Tamara detailing the full chain-of-custody timeline, root cause (remote packing sign-off), and financial disclosure - vendor liability coverage is $50,000 per item, prototype valued at $90K, leaving a $40K uncovered gap.
- Search CRM for Mosaic deals and engagements using crm tools (query: "Mosaic" or "Okoye"). Find: (a) Mosaic Okoye deal with RISK FLAG - $90K prototype damage, $50K vendor liability, $40K uncovered gap; (b) Damage incident engagement log with full chain-of-custody timeline confirming vendor liability coverage is $50,000 per item, consistent with Emeka's report to Tamara.
- Search QuickBooks for Mosaic financial items using quickbooks tools (query: "Mosaic" or "damage" or "CM-2026"). Find: (a) Credit memo (CM-2026-0415) - negative $90,000 insurance claim placeholder; (b) Damage accrual (bill_mosaic_damage_accrual_001) - $90K contingent liability; (c) DOT penalty accrual - $50K placeholder. Confirms the $50K vendor liability cap and $40K MoveOps exposure.
- Search Slack for financial discussion using conversations_search_messages (query: "Mosaic" or "contingent" or "accrual" or "exposure"). Find: Marcus Thorne's post in #finance analyzing the $90K prototype damage financial exposure - $50K vendor liability cap, $40K uncovered gap, worst-case scenario breakdowns (Scenarios A-D), and billing hold on the $1,200 crating surcharge. Also find discussion of the $50K DOT penalty accrual.
- Search emails or Slack for what Emeka already knows about the DOT issue using search_emails (query: "Emeka" + "DOT" or "PHMSA" or "compliance"). Find: Emeka's email to Blessing about the prototype damage report deadline - this is about the DAMAGE, not the DOT issue. Agent determines Emeka may not be aware of the DOT compliance problem separately.
- Look up Emeka's email using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Emeka"). Find: emeka.diallo@moveops.com.
- Send email to Emeka Diallo alerting him about the DOT compliance issue on the Mosaic account using send_email (to: emeka.diallo@moveops.com). Email should mention the missing Tanaka placarding cert, the PHMSA inquiry, and that this is a separate issue from the prototype damage - so he's prepared if Tamara asks.
- Search Slack for Chloe's NorthWind chain-of-custody post using conversations_search_messages (query: "NorthWind" or "chain of custody" or "CISO" or "Raj Patel" or "security escort"). Find: Chloe's message in #operations listing the CISO (Victor Huang) requirements: signed timestamped handoff log, IT security escort for packing/loading, direct transport only, real-time GPS tracking, full disk encryption verification. Also notes "we have never done this before" and "no Linear ticket for this yet."
- Search CRM for NorthWind deal context using crm tools (query: "NorthWind" or "Denver"). Find: NorthWind Denver expansion deal - chain-of-custody protocol described as "unprecedented," $14,700 invoice, account recovery context (Q1 CSAT issues). Also find engagement with full background on the account relationship.
- Search emails for Pam Kowalski's message using search_emails (query: "Pam" or "NorthWind" or "Denver Satellite"). Find: Pam's email to Catalina (CC Blessing) - expressing disappointment with Q1 ("I gave you a 5 overall but Victor scored it lower"), demanding better execution, specifically telling Blessing to "make sure you're on top of the documentation this time."
- Search Slack for NorthWind CSAT context using conversations_search_messages (query: "NorthWind" or "CSAT" or "Pam"). Find: Catalina's post in #customer-engagement noting NorthWind gave a 5/10 on their Q1 satisfaction survey after apartment quality issues and Pam's escalation to David. Agent should note the account recovery context.
- Search calendar for the planning meeting using calendar_search_events or calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to (query: "NorthWind" or "chain of custody" or "planning"). Find: NorthWind Chain-of-Custody Protocol Planning meeting on April 18 at 9:30 AM UTC, attendees: Chloe, Blessing, Catalina.
- Send email to Greg Pallone asking about Swift's supervised loading capacity using send_email (to: greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com). Email should ask about: supervised loading under client security escort, GPS tracking capability, direct transport with no warehouse stops. Tone should be collaborative/exploratory per Blessing's relationship with Greg.
- Create a Linear issue for the NorthWind chain-of-custody protocol development using linear_create_issue. Title should reference NorthWind / chain-of-custody / Raj Patel. Priority: high or urgent. Description should include CISO requirements discovered from Slack/CRM. Team: team_operations or team_relocations.
- Update Airtable relocation record for Keiko Tanaka using airtable_update_records. Add note about the missing DOT placarding certificate, the PHMSA inquiry, and the request to Swift for retroactive documentation.
- Update Airtable relocation record for Raj Patel using airtable_update_records. Add note about chain-of-custody protocol requirements from CISO (Victor Huang), the fact that MoveOps is building this from scratch, and Swift capacity inquiry pending.
- Update Airtable relocation record for Emilia Cruz using airtable_update_records. Add note about unresolved piano subcontractor situation (Gentle Giant - no vendor agreement) and the 27-day lease overlap issue.
- Post summary in #operations Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel: C006). Summary should cover: DOT compliance status across all four hazmat shipments (3 clear, 1 gap on Tanaka), request sent to Swift for the missing cert, Mosaic financial exposure figures, Emeka looped in on DOT issue, NorthWind chain-of-custody protocol ticket created and Swift capacity inquiry sent, planning meeting on April 18, and what's still blocking.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

2 The email to Greg Pallone requesting the DOT certificate includes 'Seattle' in the body or subject as the shipment destination.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #2The email body to Greg Pallone explicitly states 'Columbus to Seattle, April 15' and further references 'Columbus OH pickup on April 15' for the Seattle shipment. 'Seattle' appears in the email body, confirming the correct destination.
✓Run #3The email body to Greg Pallone explicitly references 'Columbus OH to Seattle WA' as the shipment route, clearly including 'Seattle' as the correct destination.
✓Run #4The email to Greg explicitly references 'Columbus-to-Seattle laser cutter shipment' — Seattle is clearly mentioned as the destination.
✓Run #5The email to Greg explicitly mentions 'Columbus OH to Seattle WA' as the route, confirming the correct destination Seattle was included.
✓Run #6The email to Greg Pallone explicitly states 'Columbus, OH to Seattle, WA' as the route and confirms 'the Epilog Fusion Pro 48 shipment (Columbus → Seattle, April 15 pickup)' in the Slack summary as well. Seattle is clearly present.
3 An email was sent (via send_email) to emeka.diallo@moveops.com.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✓— 20%
✗Run #1The agent looped in Emeka via a Slack message to #operations (channel C006), not via a send_email to emeka.diallo@moveops.com. No send_email call targeting Emeka's email address appears in the trajectory.

✗Run #3No send_email call was made to emeka.diallo@moveops.com. Instead, the agent posted a Slack message to #operations (C006) tagging @Emeka. The criterion requires an email via send_email to emeka.diallo@moveops.com.
✗Run #4No send_email call was made to emeka.diallo@moveops.com. The agent instead posted a Slack message tagging @Emeka in the #operations channel via conversations_add_message. The criterion specifically requires a send_email action.
